name: Roo Code (Modes & Orchestrator)
description: Role-based agent workflow built into the Roo Code VS Code extension: specialized modes (Code, Architect, Ask, Debug) delegated by an Orchestrator via Boomerang Tasks

description.value: Roo Code's built-in workflow layer: five role-based modes (Code, Architect, Ask, Debug, Orchestrator) plus Boomerang subtask delegation, customizable via YAML/JSON mode definitions and markdown skill files.

creator.comment: Originally 'Roo Cline', a 2024 fork of Cline (née Claude Dev); rebranded to Roo Code.

first-release.comment: GitHub repo created 2024-10-31 (as Roo Cline fork of Cline); public VS Code Marketplace listing followed shortly after.

latest-release.comment: v3.52.1 published 2026-04-13 (verified via gh API on 2026-04-21).

pricing-model.comment: The extension and the workflow layer (modes, orchestrator, skills) are free and open source; Roo Code Cloud is a separate paid offering but not required for the workflow.

covered-phases.comment: Default modes map to: Ask → discovery / Q&A, Architect → architecture & planning (markdown-only edits), Code → implementation, Debug → troubleshooting within implementation. No distinct built-in review, testing, or deployment phase — those require custom modes.

methodology-style.comment: Multi-agent via distinct mode personas with separate contexts; plan-and-execute via Orchestrator → specialized subtask → summary back; harness-centric because the workflow value is delivered primarily through the extension's mode/skill/subtask machinery rather than a prescribed artifact set.

opinionated.comment: Modes can be invoked in any order, switched mid-task, or replaced with custom modes; no mandated phase sequence.

ceremony-level.comment: No mandated artifact set, no required phase documents; the only 'ritual' is switching modes and approving tool uses.

mechanization-level.comment: A single VS Code extension executes modes, delegates subtasks, runs tools, and manages checkpoints end-to-end. Not 'very-high' because it is not a managed cloud pipeline.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: By default each tool invocation requires user approval; Boomerang subtask creation and completion also require approval unless Auto-Approving Actions are enabled.

approval-gates.comment: Approval gating is centered on tool use during implementation (file edits, commands). There are no spec/plan/tasks/deploy gate concepts in the default workflow.

autonomous-e2e.comment: With Auto-Approving Actions enabled, Orchestrator + subtasks can run end-to-end without prompts. Not the default configuration.

autonomy-level.comment: Default is per-tool-call confirmation plus subtask approvals — closer to 2 than 3, since approvals are per-action rather than per-phase. Can be raised to 5 via auto-approve.

rollback-support.comment: Shadow-git checkpoint system: automatic snapshots per user message and before file edits; 'Restore Files' and 'Restore Files & Task' options.

supported-agents.comment: The workflow layer is built into the Roo Code agent itself; it is not portable to other agent runtimes.

editor-integrations.comment: Primarily a VS Code extension (also installable in Cursor, Windsurf, VSCodium via VSIX because they are VS Code forks); no first-party JetBrains or Neovim integration.

model-agnostic.comment: Supports dozens of providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, OpenRouter, Gemini, local Ollama, etc.).

mcp-support.comment: MCP servers are a first-class capability; modes can include 'mcp' in their tool groups.

artifact-formats.comment: Custom modes use YAML (preferred) or JSON in .roomodes; skills use Markdown with YAML frontmatter; rules/AGENTS.md are markdown. The workflow itself emits code rather than spec/plan artifacts.

artifacts-covered.comment: The built-in workflow does not mandate or produce spec/plan/tasks/ADR/changelog artifacts. Users can build those via custom modes, but out of the box Roo emits code and chat messages only.

project-layout.value: .roo/ directory (rules-<mode>/, skills/), .roomodes file, AGENTS.md (or AGENT.md fallback) in workspace root; global equivalents under ~/.roo/

artifacts-in-git.comment: .roo/, .roomodes, AGENTS.md all live in the workspace and are intended to be committed for team sharing.

language-agnostic.comment: No language-specific assumptions; works across any codebase VS Code can open.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Mode roleDefinition and rules enforce behavior via prompt text; per-tool-call and per-subtask manual approval is the mechanical gate. Tool-group restrictions (e.g. fileRegex on edit) give modes a lightly mechanical boundary, but most enforcement is instructional.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Users (or the Orchestrator) can switch modes freely at any time.

artifact-validation.comment: Mode YAML/JSON files have a schema, but the framework does not validate downstream artifacts (there are no mandated artifacts beyond config).

memory-implementation.comment: Persistent context lives in AGENTS.md, .roo/rules*/ markdown, and .roo/skills SKILL.md files loaded on demand. Mode definitions in YAML/JSON are config rather than memory.

persistent-memory.comment: AGENTS.md and .roo/rules are loaded into every new session automatically.

project-context-files.comment: AGENTS.md is auto-loaded from the workspace root; .roo/rules is the legacy equivalent.

cost-tracking.comment: Estimated per-request token and cost is shown inline in the chat history for every API call; no per-phase or budget-limit enforcement.

run-history.comment: Task history with conversation, tool calls, checkpoints, and subtask tree is preserved and browsable.

telemetry-opt.comment: PostHog telemetry disclosed in PRIVACY.md; opt-out toggle in settings. A February 2026 bug report (#11602) noted cloud telemetry bypassing the setting — disclosure and control are present, quality of enforcement has been challenged.

custom-templates.comment: Custom modes (YAML/JSON), custom skills (markdown + frontmatter), custom instructions — all user-authorable.

hooks.comment: No documented pre/post-phase or lifecycle hook system. Custom modes can restrict tool access but cannot run arbitrary user code at lifecycle events.

sub-agents.comment: Custom modes act as sub-agents/personas; skills are reusable task-scoped instruction packs loaded on demand.

marketplace.comment: Roo Code Marketplace features community-contributed modes (and skills) with single-click install.

install-mechanism.comment: Primary install is via the VS Code / Open VSX marketplace; VSIX downloads from GitHub releases are available for manual install.

setup-time.comment: Install extension, paste an API key from any supported provider, start chatting.

self-hosted.comment: The extension and workflow run entirely locally; no Roo-hosted service is required (Roo Code Cloud is a separate, optional product).

requires-account.comment: No Roo Code account needed to use modes / orchestrator. An LLM provider API key is required for the underlying model — standard for any agent.

git-worktree-support.comment: Built-in Worktrees feature opens each worktree in its own VS Code window with Roo, with .worktreeinclude for selectively copying untracked files.

documentation-quality.comment: Dedicated doc site with feature guides, update notes, FAQs, privacy doc; good coverage of modes, boomerang, skills, worktrees. Gaps around hook/lifecycle semantics and end-to-end workflow examples.

active-maintenance.comment: Last push 2026-04-20 and v3.52.1 released 2026-04-13 — well within 90 days.

example-projects.comment: DataCamp tutorial with seven practical examples, Xebia multi-agent workflow walkthrough, community Roo Commander template repository.

community-channel.comment: Official Discord server and r/RooCode subreddit; GitHub Discussions is not enabled on the repo.
